title: session-start.py and doctor.py never surface which host they resolved — a dormant Codex install is indistinguishable from a working one at the one place a maintainer would look
evidence: session-start.py's main() does `host = hostapi.load_host()` (line 541) and only ever reads two capability flags off it (`host.has_statusline` at line 556, `host.plugin_root()` at line 543) — `host.name` is read nowhere in the function. The printed '=== codeArbiter startup state ===' block (line 589 onward: stage, open questions, task board, provenance drift, update notice, briefing) contains no line naming the resolved host. doctor.py similarly calls `hostapi.load_host().plugin_root()` (doctor.py:54) but its check functions (check_interpreters/check_payload/check_repo/check_statusline, lines 57-195) never call load_host().name or print it; the final results table and verdict line (184-194) name no host either.
impact: This branch's own commit history (e19778c, cited in inventory.md as evidence the silent-un-enforcement failure mode already happened once) was a hooks.json registration gap on Codex that went unnoticed until fixed post-hoc. Neither of the two places a maintainer is most likely to check 'is codeArbiter actually active as Codex right now' — the SessionStart startup-state banner (which already loads the Host object) and /ca:doctor's static-check report (which also already loads it) — prints the resolved host name. A completely dormant Codex install (missing hooks.json entry, a _host.py that silently fell back to Claude defaults per observability-002, or hooks simply never wired) produces startup/doctor output that looks structurally identical to a healthy one, because the one differentiating fact already sitting in memory (`host.name`) is never emitted.
recommendation: Add a single low-noise line to both surfaces once the Host object is loaded — e.g. session-start.py's startup-state banner gets `host: codex` (or claude) alongside `stage: ...`, and doctor.py's check_payload/verdict block adds an OK line naming the resolved host and confirming _host.py loaded without falling back to defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Running the Codex session-start hook against a real Codex environment prints a line identifying the active host as 'codex', not 'claude' or nothing.
/ca:doctor's output, run from the ca-codex payload, names the host it resolved and distinguishes a successful CodexHost load from a silent Host() fallback (ties to observability-002's breadcrumb).
